title: Whrr
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Droid |type= |image= |name=Whrr |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]''[[Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive]]'' |birth= |death= |creator= |manufacturer= |line= |model=[[Librarian droid/Legends|Librarian droid]] |class=[[Class three droid/Legends|Class three droid]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine programming]] |pronouns=new |sensor= |plating= |armament= |equipment= |affiliation=[[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] }} {{Quote|You are reading faster. That's good!''"
"''Why is that good?''"
"''You can read more books!|Whrr and Boba Fett, talking through the library slot|}} '''Whrr''' was a [[Librarian droid/Legends|librarian droid]] with [[Gender/Legends|masculine programming]] that worked during [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] in a small [[Library/Legends|library]] [[Tipoca City library branch|branch]] on [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]], located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. Young [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] frequently visited the library branch because his [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|father]] was on Kamino due to his occupation as the [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|new clone army]], produced by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]]. Before Fett left Kamino with his father after the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tracked Jango Fett there due to his association with the [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|assassination attempts]] on [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala's]] life, Boba Fett traded in his latest batch of [[Book/Legends|books]] from the library to the droid, and inadvertently gave Whrr a [[Holobook/Legends|holobook]] containing [[Jango Fett's code|advice]] from his father. Upon returning to Kamino to fetch the book after his father's [[Death/Legends|death]], Whrr gave the holobook back to Fett, along with 250 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for keeping a book that did not belong to the branch. ==Biography== {{Quote|There's money due on this book. Quite a bit.''"
"''It's not even really a book. Besides, I didn't check it out. It's mine! I left it with you.''"
"''Exactly. Which means the library owes you, let's see, two hundred and fifty credits.|Whrr and Boba Fett, discussing the latter's holobook|}} Whrr worked in a small library branch in [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]], during [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. The branch had a bell to ring and a slot in the doorway, through which [[Book/Legends|books]] and [[Hologame/Legends|hologames]] could be passed, and due to the use of that slot, Whrr was never seen by the users of the library. Whrr became friends with the young [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], and encouraged him to read while at the same [[Time/Legends|time]] dissuading him from hologames. The young boy was staying with his father [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who held the position of the [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] for the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|new army]], produced by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|natives]] of Kamino. Boba Fett came to the library regularly, and once did so after trying to free one of five [[Sea-mouse|sea mice]] his father had him feed to Jango's [[sea eel]] while the latter was on a [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala|mission]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],{{Film|II}} only to have the freed sea-mouse [[Death/Legends|eaten]] instantly. Whrr consoled the young Fett before complimenting the boy on his improved reading skills and passing new books for Fett to read through the slot. However, Jango's mission soon caught up to him and his son. [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], discovered the existence of Kamino, whose coordinates had been erased from the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]]' memory, and also discovered Jango Fett, who the Jedi suspected as the culprit to the mission. Jango Fett decided they should leave Kamino to avoid Jedi scrutiny, and Boba had only a few minutes to return the latest batch of library books to Whrr lest he be charged for overdue books. Boba hurriedly shoved the books through the slot, and as he did, a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Holobook/Legends|holobook]] containing [[Jango Fett's code|advice]] was sent through with it. Whrr noticed that book, and tried to tell Boba, but the young boy was in too much of a hurry to get back to his father, and left without the book. Boba Fett later returned to Kamino in search of the book, as it contained important information from his father, who had [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|perished]] shortly after leaving Kamino on the planet [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] at the hands of another Jedi, [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], leaving Boba Fett an orphan. Boba Fett was desperate for the information left for him by his father, and soon was at the then closed library branch, calling for Whrr to help him. Whrr made an awning slide out to protect Fett from the [[Water/Legends|rain]] that typically fell on Kamino, and proceeded to listen to Fett's account of the events leading to his return to Whrr, including the fact that Fett was now an orphan. Fett asked for his holobook, which Whrr gave to him, along with a 250 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] fee for keeping one of Fett's books after realizing the young boy had no money to survive on his own. Whrr bade farewell to him, and, as Fett left, made a snuffling sound close to crying. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Life is hard on the weak and small, I guess.''"
"''What do you mean, you guess? Don't you know?''"
"''Not really. That's why I stay in here, out of the way.|Whrr and Boba Fett, talking shortly after the sea-mouse incident|}} A [[Librarian droid/Legends|librarian droid]] with [[Gender/Legends|masculine programming]], Whrr was classified as a [[Class three droid/Legends|class three droid]] because he provided services to the community. Whrr made whirring and clicking sounds as he performed tasks inside the [[Library/Legends|library]] [[Tipoca City library branch|branch]]. He also could make a snuffling noise close to crying, and had a change-the-subject noise. Whrr became the young Boba Fett's friend due to his visits to Whrr's branch, and the friendship extended far enough to let Fett tell Whrr his problems. When Fett left for the last time, Whrr made a snuffling sound close to crying. As a [[Librarian/Legends|librarian]], Whrr disapproved of hologames, instead urging Fett to find the pictures and images in the books, not the hologames. Whrr monitored Fett's reading level as the young boy regularly took books from the library. One of the reasons Whrr stayed inside the library branch was that he was not certain about things, such as life being hard on the small and weak. He also lacked a sense of humor, and was offended when Fett told him if Whrr would have his way, everybody would read books. Whrr was also observant, and noticed quickly when Fett accidentally gave Whrr the holobook along with his other due books. Whrr was also good at understanding Fett's problems, and understood immediately that Fett was in trouble when Fett told him the events that led up to him being orphaned. Whrr was concerned, and though under the guise of a fee, helped Fett out as best he could with 250 credits. ==Behind the scenes== Whrr first appeared in the novel ''[[Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive]]'', written by [[Terry Bisson]] and released on [[April 23]], [[2002]]. Due to the fact that the book is written from Boba Fett's point of view, he does not see Whrr, only talking to him through the slot. After hearing the clicking and whirring sounds Whrr made, Fett figures that Whrr was a droid. This article is written based on the assumption that Fett's figuring is right. ==Appearances== *''[[Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Whrr }} [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:Kamino residents]] [[Category:Librarian droids]]
